senecAccountInfo: Enter your mein-senec.de credentials here. These are shared by the SENEC App API and mein-senec.de features.
controlConsentInfo: Enable the control connectors you want to use. Each connector requires its own consent. Features available via multiple connectors can only be controlled by one at a time.
controlFeatureInfo: Configure which features to control and which connector handles them.
controlWallboxConnectorHelp: Select which connector should handle wallbox control. Only one connector can be active at a time to avoid conflicts. Experimental feature — please report your experience to the developer.
controlSocketsConnectorHelp: Select which connector should handle switchable socket control. Only available if your SENEC system has switchable sockets configured.
connectorWarnLocal: ⚠ Local connection (lala.cgi) is not enabled! Enable it in the Local Connection tab first.
connectorWarnApi: ⚠ SENEC App API is not enabled! Enable it in the SENEC App API tab first.
connectorWarnWeb: ⚠ mein-senec.de is not enabled! Enable it in the mein-senec.de tab first.
controlSocketsForceHelp: Enable socket control even if your system does not report socket capability. Use this if you have sockets but the feature is not detected (e.g. missing license). This is untested — use at your own risk and please report your experience to the developer.
activeWebControlHelp: Available controls depend on your system's capabilities. You accept any potential risks incurred by activating this option.
webControlInfo: Available controls (emergency power reserve, peak shaving, SG-Ready) are created automatically based on your system's capabilities. Control datapoints appear under 'control.EmergencyPower', 'control.PeakShaving', and 'control.SGReady'.
customUserAgentHelp: Only used when User-Agent mode is set to 'Custom'.
userAgentModeHelp:
Choose which User-Agent should be sent for outbound HTTP requests.
Browser = use a common browser User-Agent.
Custom = use the User-Agent defined in the field to the right.
Integration = The user agent reveals the integration via ioBroker, including the adapter version.
Revert to 'Browser' if you experience problems with the User-Agent.
apiConcurrencyStartHelp: Here you can define how many API requests the adapter should send at the same time when starting up. [min 1, max 4]. Default = 1. Higher values can speed up data retrieval but also increase the risk of API errors. If you experience API errors, please try to lower this value.
apiConcurrencyMaxHelp: Here you can define how many API requests the adapter can send at the same time max. [min 1, max 6]. Default = 1. Higher values can speed up data retrieval but also increase the risk of API errors. If you experience API errors, please try to lower this value.
dbgShowPollingHelp: Promotes polling status messages from debug to info level. Shows when the adapter polls each connector and schedules the next poll.
dbgReqRespHelp: Logs HTTP request URLs and response details at debug level. Only recommended for debugging. May log sensitive information — do not leave enabled permanently.
dbgDebugLogHelp: Promotes queue concurrency recommendations and statistics from debug to info level. Only available for connectors with request queues (API, mein-senec).
dbgDebugStatesHelp: Writes queue diagnostics and debug data to dedicated ioBroker states. States are automatically cleaned up when this option is turned off. Only available for connectors with request queues (API, mein-senec).
controlReboot: Allow appliance reboot via control datapoint
controlRebootHelp: When activated it is possible to reboot the SENEC appliance via 'control.RebootAppliance' datapoint. This is normally not available via web interface.
controlSocketsHelp: lala.cgi connection required! When activated, switchable sockets (schaltbare Kontakte) can be controlled via 'control.Sockets' datapoints. Only available if your SENEC system has switchable sockets configured.
controlWallboxHelp: lala.cgi connection required! When activated, wallbox charging can be controlled via 'control.Wallbox' datapoints. Control datapoints are only created if your system has wallboxes configured. WARNING: If both local and API wallbox control are enabled, avoid sending conflicting commands from both at the same time — this could lead to unexpected wallbox behavior. Experimental feature — please report your experience to the developer.
activeApiControlHelp: You accept any potential risks incurred by activating this option.
controlApiWallboxHelp: When activated, wallbox lock, charging mode, and charge settings can be controlled via 'control.api' datapoints. Only available if your system has wallboxes discovered via the SENEC App API. WARNING: If both local and API wallbox control are enabled, avoid sending conflicting commands from both at the same time — this could lead to unexpected wallbox behavior. Experimental feature — please report your experience to the developer.
useLalaHelp: Connect to SENEC appliance via local network. This requires access to local monitoring.
controlOverviewLabel: Which controls are available via which connector?
controlOverviewLocal: 🟢 Local (lala.cgi): Force charging · Reboot · Sockets · Wallbox
controlOverviewWeb: 🟠 Web (mein-senec.de): Emergency power · Peak shaving · SG-Ready · Sockets (incl. name)
controlOverviewNote: Sockets and wallbox can only be controlled by one connector at a time.
activeControlHelp: lala.cgi connection required. You accept any potential risks incurred by activating this option possibly including loss of warranty.
IPHelp: Here you enter the IP or FQDN of your SENEC System
batteryCapacityHelp: Total battery capacity in kWh. Used for time estimates in the web dashboard. Set to 0 for auto-detect from API (requires SENEC App API connector). Set manually if only using local or web connector.
PollIntervalHelp: Here you can define the polling interval for high priority data [min 1, max 3600 seconds]. Default = 10.
PollIntervalLowHelp: Here you can define the polling interval for low priority data [min 10, max 3600 minutes]. Default = 60.
RequestTimeoutHelp: Heare you define the request timeout when polling from SENEC [min 1000, max 100000]. Default = 5000.
PollRetriesHelp: Here you enter how often you want the adapter to retry polling in case of error [min 0, max 999]. 0 = never, 999 = unlimited. Default = 10.
PollRetryFactorHelp: Here you enter how you want to space retries apart of each other. n'th retry will happen after Interval * Multiplier * n seconds. [min 1, max 10]. Default = 2.
HighPrioDataPointsDisclaimer: I hereby acknowledge that it is in my very own responsibility alone to ensure these settings are valid. I also acknowledge that problematic settings can cause my SENEC appliance to malfunction and that I willingly accept these risks.
HighPrioDataPointsDisclaimerHelp: Wrong settings or even requesting too many datapoints can cause the SENEC appliance to reboot, malfunction and other undesired behavior. If you don't understand what these settings are or how to properly configure them, please leave them all empty!
HighPrioBMSHelp: Auto-Added: CELL_TEMPERATURES_MODULE_A, CELL_TEMPERATURES_MODULE_B, CELL_TEMPERATURES_MODULE_C, CELL_TEMPERATURES_MODULE_D, CELL_VOLTAGES_MODULE_A, CELL_VOLTAGES_MODULE_B, CELL_VOLTAGES_MODULE_C, CELL_VOLTAGES_MODULE_D, CURRENT, SOC, SYSTEM_SOC, TEMP_MAX, TEMP_MIN, VOLTAGE
HighPrioENERGYHelp: Auto-Added: STAT_STATE, GUI_BAT_DATA_POWER, GUI_INVERTER_POWER, GUI_HOUSE_POW, GUI_GRID_POW, GUI_BAT_DATA_FUEL_CHARGE, GUI_CHARGING_INFO, GUI_BOOSTING_INFO, GUI_BAT_DATA_POWER, GUI_BAT_DATA_VOLTAGE, GUI_BAT_DATA_CURRENT, GUI_BAT_DATA_FUEL_CHARGE, GUI_BAT_DATA_OA_CHARGING, STAT_LIMITED_NET_SKEW, SAFE_CHARGE_FORCE, SAFE_CHARGE_PROHIBIT, SAFE_CHARGE_RUNNING
HighPrioPMOBJHelp: Auto-Added: FREQ, U_AC, I_AC, P_AC, P_TOTAL
USE_SENEC_API_HELP: Check if the adapter shall make use of the SENEC API.
Email-Help: Email-Address used to login to mein-senec.de
Password_Help:: Password used to login to mein-senec.de
TOTPSecretHelp: Only needed if two-factor authentication (2FA) is enabled on your mein-senec.de account. Enter the base32 secret key shown when setting up 2FA (the text key behind the QR code, not the 6-digit code). Leave empty if 2FA is not enabled.
PollIntervalAPIHelp: Here you can define the polling interval [min 3, max 1440 minutes] for Dashboard. Default = 6. SENEC updates API Data every 5 minutes. Setting the interval to 6 minutes is recommended to get the most up-to-date data without putting too much load on the API or errors.
PollIntervalAPIDetailsHelp: Here you can define the polling interval [min 10, max 1440 minutes] for detailed data. Default = 60. This includes data for hour and day that can therefore be polled less frequently. Setting it to 60 minutes is recommended to get the most up-to-date data without putting too much load on the API or errors.
PollIntervalAPIHeavyHelp: Here you can define the polling interval [min 720, max 2880 minutes] for heavy data. Default = 1440 (once per day). This includes data for month and year that can therefore be polled less frequently. Setting it to 1440 minutes is recommended to get the most up-to-date data without putting too much load on the API or errors.
ReBuildHistoryHelp: Off = Normal mode, Resume = Continue rebuild with existing data, Full = Re-Fetch all data from API again before resuming. Use Full if you think local data is erraneous. (Up to two adapter restarts will happen for Full and Resume!)
ReBuildHistoryStartYearHelp: This usually is the year your system was put in place. You can go back until 2009 but every year you don't have data will show empty yearly measurements folders. Default: 2025, Range [2009..current Year]
USE_MEIN_SENEC_HELP: Enable polling and control via the mein-senec.de web portal. Uses the same credentials as the SENEC App API. Provides spare capacity, peak shaving, and SG-Ready control. Currently in debug/discovery mode.
webPollIntervalStatusHelp: Polling interval for status overview (live power data). [min 3, max 60 minutes]. Default = 6.
webPollIntervalMediumHelp: Polling interval for spare capacity, SG-Ready state, sockets, and measurements (today, yesterday, autarky). [min 60, max 1440 minutes]. Default = 360 (6 hours).
webPollIntervalSlowHelp: Polling interval for peak shaving settings, SG-Ready config, and heavy measurements (monthly, yearly, AllTime totals). [min 360, max 2880 minutes]. Default = 1440 (daily).
webMeasurementsHelp: Poll measurement history (today, yesterday, 7 days, monthly, yearly, AllTime) and autarky from mein-senec.de. Data appears under '_meinsenec.Measurements' and '_meinsenec.Autarky'. Uses the configured polling tiers for scheduling.
webMeasurementsDetailHelp: ⚠ WARNING: This creates ~3,500 additional states for 5-minute resolution data (today + yesterday × 6 measurement types × ~288 data points each). Only enable if you need fine-grained intra-day data for charting. Hourly aggregations and daily totals are always available regardless of this setting. Disabling this option will delete all existing detail states.
webConcurrencyStartHelp: Number of parallel requests to mein-senec.de at startup. [min 1, max 3]. Default = 1. Higher values speed up polling but may cause rate-limiting.
webConcurrencyMaxHelp: Maximum number of parallel requests to mein-senec.de. [min 1, max 4]. Default = 2. The queue auto-adjusts based on success rate.
webMinRequestIntervalHelp: Minimum time between consecutive requests to mein-senec.de. [min 400, max 5000 ms]. Default = 500. Increase if you experience rate limiting.
apiMinRequestIntervalHelp: Minimum time between consecutive API requests. [min 400, max 5000 ms]. Default = 400. Increase if you experience rate limiting or API errors.
USE_SENEC_CONNECT_HELP: Enable polling via the official SENEC.Connect API. Requires a SENEC.Connect subscription (paid product) and a V4/E4 system. Uses the same SENEC account but a separate subscription key. Older systems (V2/V3) are not supported by SENEC.Connect.
connectSubscriptionKeyHelp: Your SENEC.Connect subscription key (Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key). Found under your profile at developer.senec.com after subscribing to the SENEC.Connect product.
connectSubscriptionInfo:
To obtain a subscription key:
1. Go to developer.senec.com
2. Sign in with your mein-senec.de credentials
3. Browse to the SENEC.Connect product and click Subscribe
4. Find your subscription key under Profile → My Subscriptions
5. Copy the key and paste it above
connectPollIntervalHelp: Here you can define the polling interval [min 60, max 86400 seconds]. Default = 300 (5 minutes). SENEC.Connect allows approx. 45,000 requests per month (~1/minute). Going below 60 seconds is not recommended and may exhaust your quota.
connectIncludeHelp: Comma-separated list of data sections to include: battery, meter, evse, bessNameplate. Leave as default to get all available data.
